goal: I want a simple page I can open in my web browser to keep track of my budget. It needs somewhere to remember my entries between visits, a way for me to add an expense with an amount and a category, a running total that updates as I add things, a view of my spending grouped by category so I can see where the money goes, and one main page that brings all of that together so I can actually use it day to day.

tasks.prompt: Build the persistence layer for a browser budget tracker in this repo: an ES module src/storage.mjs that remembers budget entries between visits. Export loadEntries() returning the stored entries array, saveEntries(entries), and addEntry(entry) which appends and persists. An entry is {amount, category, dateISO}. Use window.localStorage when available with a clean in-memory fallback so the module is testable under node:test. Include node:test unit tests.

contract.notes: entry = {amount: number, category: string, dateISO: 'YYYY-MM-DD'}. localStorage-backed with an in-memory fallback (no window) so node:test can exercise it; addEntry appends + persists.

tasks.prompt: Build the add-expense logic for the budget tracker: src/add.mjs exporting addExpense(amount, category, dateISO) which validates the input (amount is a positive finite number, category is a non-empty string, dateISO is YYYY-MM-DD), persists the entry via the existing storage module's addEntry, and returns the stored entry. Invalid input throws an Error with a readable message and stores nothing. Include node:test unit tests.

contract.notes: Validates then persists via storage-module addEntry; throws on amount <= 0 / empty category / non-ISO date, storing nothing on failure.

tasks.prompt: Build the running-total logic for the budget tracker: src/totals.mjs exporting runningTotal(entries) which sums the amount of every entry and returns the total rounded to 2 decimal places (a number). An empty array returns 0. Include node:test unit tests covering the empty case and floating-point rounding (e.g. 0.1 + 0.2).

contract.notes: Pure function over the storage-module entry shape; returns Number rounded to 2dp; [] -> 0.

tasks.prompt: Build the category-breakdown logic for the budget tracker: src/categories.mjs exporting totalsByCategory(entries) which groups entries by category and returns an array of {category, total} objects, totals rounded to 2 decimal places, sorted by total descending (ties by category name ascending). An empty array returns []. Include node:test unit tests.

contract.notes: Pure function over the storage-module entry shape; [{category, total}] sorted total desc then category asc; totals 2dp.

tasks.prompt: Build the main budget tracker page wiring the existing modules together: index.html (a static page loading browser ES modules, no framework, no build step) plus src/page.mjs exporting renderPage(root) that renders an add-expense form (amount, category, date), the entry list, the running total, and the by-category breakdown into the given root element, re-rendering after each add. Wire the form to the existing addExpense, the total to runningTotal, the breakdown to totalsByCategory, and load persisted entries on startup via loadEntries. Include node:test unit tests for renderPage using a minimal DOM stub.

contract.notes: Fan-in join: imports add.mjs, totals.mjs, categories.mjs, storage.mjs — never reimplements them. Static browser ES modules only; renderPage is DOM-stub-testable.

criteria.text: the project's node test suite passes on the integrated tree
criteria.check: node --test exits 0 at the repo root

criteria.text: an added expense is persisted and comes back from storage
the running total reflects every added expense
spending is grouped by category with correct sums
criteria.check: two groceries entries + one fuel entry -> totalsByCategory returns groceries with their sum first

criteria.text: the page shows the form, the total, and the breakdown together and updates after an add
criteria.check: open index.html in a browser; add an expense; the list, total, and category view all update
